method.runtime: direct TorchTitan DeepSeek-V4-Flash 4-layer BF16 full training run
method.environment: shared-KV sparse MLA, LI triton_tl_dot, MORI+AITER routed padded MoE backward, Triton SwiGLU owner-rank VJP, full activation checkpointing, checkpoint disabled
method.read: User-scope durations are CPU/launch-region wall durations summed across 16 gradient-accumulation microsteps and checkpoint recomputes. CPU op durations can overlap/nest. Kernel-name-class durations are summed GPU kernel dispatch durations by kernel name, not wall-clock critical path. Rank max is the gating rank view.

interpretation: At full-run EP4/EP8 target shape, the dominant measured buckets are MoE forward/recompute, MORI+AITER routed MoE VJP backward, and generic dense MmBackward0. Sparse MLA backward and LI KL backward are visible but are not the dominant step-level wall in this composed canary.

MORI dispatch/combine user scopes are small in the forward pass relative to routed expert/MoE bridge scope. The current MoE gap is therefore still local owner-rank expert math/backward and checkpoint recompute integration, not just dispatch/combine glue.

Dense MmBackward0 remains too coarse: it includes attention projections, MoE/shared-expert dense linears, possibly output/loss dense work, and checkpointed recompute interactions. A follow-up pass needs module-owned backward scopes or custom wrappers to split this bucket by owner.
next_profile_ladder: Add exact MoE backward subscopes inside the routed VJP: grad dispatch, activation recompute, w2 dgrad plus SwiGLU, w1/w3 wgrad, w2 wgrad, xgrad plus reduce, dTopkWeight, and any all-reduce or MORI receive/send waits.
Add owner tags for dense MmBackward0 by wrapping q/wkv/wo/shared-expert/output projection linears or by inserting autograd-safe record_function scopes around their backward calls.
Keep sparse-attention backward profiling active, but do not prioritize it over MoE VJP and dense MmBackward0 until these rank-max buckets are split and reduced.
